Cloudflare Stream does support video clipping functionality through their API - you can trim videos and create clips with custom start/end times, and they support live instant clipping for streams up to 60 seconds. You can also add metadata like custom watermarks and thumbnails to clipped videos.

## Partial Solutions Available

- Craft CMS has a Cloudflare Stream plugin for uploading video assets
- Umbraco has integrations with Cloudflare Stream for improved media upload processes

- JW Player provides video clipping from live events, extensive metadata editing capabilities, and CMS-like organization features
- Wistia offers custom metadata management, video organization with tags and folders, but limited clipping functionality

## The Gap in the Market
There were community requests for trimming/clipping functionality in Cloudflare Stream as recently as 2022, indicating this was a missing feature, though they've since added it via API.

## Bottom Line
You'd likely need to either:
1. Build a custom solution using Cloudflare Stream's clipping API with your own metadata management layer
2. Consider switching to Mux, which offers the most comprehensive feature set for clipping and metadata management
3. Use a hybrid approach with an existing CMS integration for Cloudflare Stream and custom development for the clipping workflow

The closest existing solution would be **Mux** if you're open to alternatives to Cloudflare Stream, as it natively supports instant clipping, rich metadata management, and CMS integrations.

summaries.summary: Analyzed Cloudflare Stream's clipping API comprehensively.

content.text:
Here's the complete breakdown of Cloudflare Stream's clipping API:

## **Core Clipping API**

### **Endpoint**
```
POST https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/accounts/{account_id}/stream/clip
```

### **Authentication**
Requires an API token with appropriate permissions

### **Basic Parameters**
The API accepts these core parameters:
- `clippedFromVideoUID`: The unique identifier for the source video
- `startTimeSeconds`: When the new clip begins (in seconds)
- `endTimeSeconds`: When the new clip ends (in seconds)

### **Basic Example**
```bash
curl --location --request POST 'https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/accounts/YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID/stream/clip' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
  "clippedFromVideoUID": "0ea62994907491cf9ebefb0a34c1e2c6",
  "startTimeSeconds": 10,
  "endTimeSeconds": 15
}'
```

## **Advanced Features & Metadata**

### **Custom Naming**
You can specify metadata including a custom name for the clipped video using the `meta` object:

```json
{
  "clippedFromVideoUID": "0ea62994907491cf9ebefb0a34c1e2c6",
  "startTimeSeconds": 10,
  "endTimeSeconds": 15,
  "meta": {
    "name": "my-custom-clip.mp4"
  }
}
```

### **Watermarks**
You can apply custom watermarks to clips:

```json
{
  "clippedFromVideoUID": "0ea62994907491cf9ebefb0a34c1e2c6",
  "startTimeSeconds": 10,
  "endTimeSeconds": 15,
  "watermark": {
    "uid": "4babd675387c3d927f58c41c761978fe"
  }
}
```

### **Access Control**
You can require signed URLs for private access:

```json
{
  "clippedFromVideoUID": "0ea62994907491cf9ebefb0a34c1e2c6",
  "startTimeSeconds": 10,
  "endTimeSeconds": 15,
  "requireSignedURLs": true
}
```

### **Custom Thumbnails**
You can specify a custom thumbnail timestamp as a percentage of the clip duration:

```json
{
  "clippedFromVideoUID": "0ea62994907491cf9ebefb0a34c1e2c6",
  "startTimeSeconds": 10,
  "endTimeSeconds": 15,
  "thumbnailTimestampPct": 0.5
}
```

## **Live Stream Clipping**

For live streams, there's a different approach with **instant clipping**:

### **Live Clip URLs**
For live streams, you can generate clips dynamically using URL parameters:
- **Clip Manifest**: `https://customer-{CODE}.cloudflarestream.com/{VIDEO_ID}/manifest/clip.m3u8?time=600s&duration=30s`
- **Clip MP4**: `https://customer-{CODE}.cloudflarestream.com/{VIDEO_ID}/clip.mp4?time=600s&duration=30s&filename=clip.mp4`

### **Live Clip Parameters**
- `time`: Start time in seconds from the beginning of the stream
- `duration`: Duration of the clip (max 60 seconds for instant clips)
- `filename`: Optional filename for the clip

## **Processing & Webhooks**

- Clipped videos are processed asynchronously (status shows "Queued" then "Ready")
- You can subscribe to webhook notifications to know when processing is complete
- Clipped videos don't inherit scheduled deletion dates from the source video

## **Limitations**

- Live instant clips are limited to 60 seconds maximum duration
- Live instant clips don't create new entries in your video library (they're generated dynamically)

content.text:
Ah, you're asking what the clipping API actually **does** functionally. Here's what it does:

## **What the Clipping API Does**

The Cloudflare Stream clipping API **creates a brand new, separate video file** from an existing video by cutting out a specific time segment.

### **The Process:**
1. **You specify:** A source video + start time + end time
2. **Cloudflare:** Creates an entirely new video asset that contains only that time segment
3. **Result:** You get a new video with its own unique ID that you can treat as a completely separate video

### **Example:**
- **Original video:** 20-minute presentation (video ID: `abc123`)
- **You clip:** seconds 300-480 (5 minutes to 8 minutes)
- **Result:** New 3-minute video (new video ID: `xyz789`) containing only that segment

### **What You Get:**
- **New video file** with its own unique video ID
- **Own playback URLs** (HLS, DASH)
- **Own thumbnail** 
- **Own metadata** (title, description, etc.)
- **Own analytics** tracking
- **Own access controls** (can be private/public independently)

### **What It's NOT:**
- ❌ It's not just adding timestamps to the same video
- ❌ It's not creating a "virtual" clip that references the original
- ❌ It doesn't modify the original video (that stays intact)

### **Key Point:**
The clipped video becomes a completely independent asset that you can share, embed, download, or manage separately from the original video. It's like taking scissors to a physical film reel and creating a new, shorter reel.

For live streams, the instant clipping works differently - those are dynamic/virtual clips that don't create new permanent video assets.

content.text:
You're absolutely right to lean towards **Django** - it's actually the better choice for your use case! Let me explain why:

## **Django vs Directus for Complex Relational Data**

### **Django Wins Because:**

**1. ORM Sophistication**
```python
# Django can handle complex relationships elegantly
class Clip(models.Model):
    video = models.ForeignKey(Video)
    start_time = models.DurationField()
    end_time = models.DurationField()
    
    # Many-to-many with through model for rich relationships
    performers = models.ManyToManyField(Person, through='Performance')
    
    def clean(self):
        # Custom validation - ensure clip is within video duration
        if self.end_time > self.video.duration:
            raise ValidationError("Clip cannot exceed video duration")

class Performance(models.Model):  # Through model
    clip = models.ForeignKey(Clip)
    person = models.ForeignKey(Person)
    role = models.CharField(max_length=100)  # "Principal", "Corps", etc.
    costume_notes = models.TextField()
```

**2. Domain-Specific Logic**
Django excels at complex business rules that archives need:
- Validate clip timestamps against video duration
- Auto-generate clip titles based on metadata
- Complex search across relationships
- Rights management workflows
- Data migration tools for legacy archives

**3. Django Admin Customization**
```python
@admin.register(Clip)
class ClipAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):
    list_display = ['title', 'video', 'duration', 'get_performers']
    list_filter = ['video__performance__venue', 'piece__choreographer']
    search_fields = ['title', 'performers__name', 'piece__title']
    
    def get_performers(self, obj):
        return ", ".join([p.name for p in obj.performers.all()])
```

**4. Data Integrity**
Django's migrations and constraints are bulletproof for archival data that needs to last decades.

## **Directus Limitations for Your Use Case:**

- **Business Logic:** Limited to basic validation rules
- **Complex Queries:** Not as powerful for cross-relationship searches
- **Customization:** Harder to customize for domain-specific workflows
- **Data Migrations:** Less sophisticated tooling

## **Recommended Django Stack:**

```
- Django + Django Admin (CMS interface)
- PostgreSQL (excellent for complex queries)
- Cloudflare Stream integration (custom model fields)
- Django REST Framework (APIs for your frontend)
- Celery (background tasks for video processing)
```

**Bottom Line:** For a dance archive with complex metadata relationships and domain-specific workflows, Django's power and flexibility outweigh Directus's convenience. You'll appreciate the ORM sophistication as your data model grows complex.

<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
    // Video state
    let currentTime = 0;
    let startTime = null;
    let endTime = null;
    let isPlaying = false;
    let videoFPS = 30; // Default, would be fetched from video metadata
    const videoDuration = {{ video.duration_seconds|default:0 }};
    
    // DOM elements
    const player = document.getElementById('video-player');
    const timeline = document.getElementById('timeline');
    const clipMarker = document.getElementById('clip-marker');
    const currentTimeDisplay = document.getElementById('current-time');
    const currentFrameDisplay = document.getElementById('current-frame');
    const clipDurationDisplay = document.getElementById('clip-duration');
    const startTimeTc = document.getElementById('start-time-tc');
    const endTimeTc = document.getElementById('end-time-tc');
    const startTimeInput = document.getElementById('start-time-input');
    const endTimeInput = document.getElementById('end-time-input');
    
    // Frame-perfect calculations
    function secondsToFrames(seconds) {
        return Math.floor(seconds * videoFPS);
    }
    
    function framesToSeconds(frames) {
        return frames / videoFPS;
    }
    
    function formatTimecode(seconds, includeFrames = true) {
        const totalFrames = Math.floor(seconds * videoFPS);
        const hours = Math.floor(seconds / 3600);
        const minutes = Math.floor((seconds % 3600) / 60);
        const secs = Math.floor(seconds % 60);
        const frames = totalFrames % videoFPS;
        
        if (includeFrames) {
            return `${hours.toString().padStart(2, '0')}:${minutes.toString().padStart(2, '0')}:${secs.toString().padStart(2, '0')}.${frames.toString().padStart(3, '0')}`;
        } else {
            const milliseconds = Math.floor((seconds % 1) * 1000);
            return `${hours.toString().padStart(2, '0')}:${minutes.toString().padStart(2, '0')}:${secs.toString().padStart(2, '0')}.${milliseconds.toString().padStart(3, '0')}`;
        }
    }
    
    function parseTimecode(timecode) {
        // Parse formats: HH:MM:SS.fff, MM:SS.fff, SS.fff, or just seconds
        if (!timecode) return 0;
        
        // If it's just a number, treat as seconds
        if (!isNaN(timecode)) return parseFloat(timecode);
        
        const parts = timecode.split(':');
        let seconds = 0;
        
        if (parts.length === 3) {
            // HH:MM:SS.fff
            seconds = parseInt(parts[0]) * 3600 + parseInt(parts[1]) * 60 + parseFloat(parts[2]);
        } else if (parts.length === 2) {
            // MM:SS.fff
            seconds = parseInt(parts[0]) * 60 + parseFloat(parts[1]);
        } else if (parts.length === 1) {
            // SS.fff
            seconds = parseFloat(parts[0]);
        }
        
        return Math.max(0, Math.min(seconds, videoDuration));
    }
    
    // Update all time displays
    function updateTimeDisplays() {
        const frame = secondsToFrames(currentTime);
        currentTimeDisplay.textContent = formatTimecode(currentTime, false);
        currentFrameDisplay.textContent = frame;
        
        // Update clip duration if both start and end are set
        if (startTime !== null && endTime !== null) {
            const duration = endTime - startTime;
            clipDurationDisplay.textContent = formatTimecode(duration, false);
        } else {
            clipDurationDisplay.textContent = '00:00:00.000';
        }
    }
    
    // Seek to specific time with frame precision
    function seekToTime(newTime) {
        // Round to nearest frame for precision
        const frameTime = framesToSeconds(secondsToFrames(newTime));
        currentTime = Math.max(0, Math.min(frameTime, videoDuration));
        
        // In a real implementation, this would communicate with the Cloudflare player
        // For now, we'll simulate the seeking
        updateTimeDisplays();
        
        // Update timeline position
        updateTimelinePosition();
    }
    
    // Frame-by-frame navigation
    function stepFrame(direction) {
        const currentFrame = secondsToFrames(currentTime);
        const newFrame = Math.max(0, Math.min(currentFrame + direction, secondsToFrames(videoDuration)));
        seekToTime(framesToSeconds(newFrame));
    }
    
    // Jump by seconds
    function jumpSeconds(seconds) {
        seekToTime(currentTime + seconds);
    }
    
    // Update timeline position indicator
    function updateTimelinePosition() {
        if (videoDuration > 0) {
            const percent = (currentTime / videoDuration) * 100;
            // Add a playhead indicator if needed
        }
    }
    
    // Update clip marker on timeline
    function updateClipMarker() {
        if (startTime !== null && endTime !== null && videoDuration > 0) {
            const startPercent = (startTime / videoDuration) * 100;
            const endPercent = (endTime / videoDuration) * 100;
            const width = endPercent - startPercent;
            
            clipMarker.style.left = startPercent + '%';
            clipMarker.style.width = width + '%';
            clipMarker.style.display = 'block';
        }
    }
    
    // Mark in/out points
    function markIn() {
        startTime = currentTime;
        startTimeInput.value = Math.floor(startTime);
        startTimeTc.value = formatTimecode(startTime, false);
        updateClipMarker();
        updateTimeDisplays();
        console.log(`In point marked at ${formatTimecode(startTime)}`);
    }
    
    function markOut() {
        endTime = currentTime;
        endTimeInput.value = Math.floor(endTime);
        endTimeTc.value = formatTimecode(endTime, false);
        updateClipMarker();
        updateTimeDisplays();
        console.log(`Out point marked at ${formatTimecode(endTime)}`);
    }
    
    // Toggle play/pause
    function togglePlayPause() {
        isPlaying = !isPlaying;
        console.log(isPlaying ? 'Playing' : 'Paused');
        // In real implementation, would control the actual video player
    }
    
    // Preview clip
    function previewClip() {
        if (startTime !== null && endTime !== null) {
            const previewUrl = `https://iframe.videodelivery.net/{{ video.cloudflare_stream_id }}?startTime=${Math.floor(startTime)}s`;
            window.open(previewUrl, '_blank');
        } else {
            alert('Please mark both start and end times first.');
        }
    }
    
    // Keyboard event handler
    function handleKeyboard(e) {
        // Don't trigger shortcuts when typing in form fields
        if (e.target.tagName === 'INPUT' || e.target.tagName === 'TEXTAREA' || e.target.tagName === 'SELECT') {
            if (e.key === 'Enter' && e.target.closest('#clip-form')) {
                e.preventDefault();
                document.getElementById('clip-form').dispatchEvent(new Event('submit'));
            }
            return;
        }
        
        switch (e.key) {
            case ' ':
                e.preventDefault();
                togglePlayPause();
                break;
            case 'i':
            case 'I':
                e.preventDefault();
                markIn();
                break;
            case 'o':
            case 'O':
                e.preventDefault();
                markOut();
                break;
            case 'p':
            case 'P':
                e.preventDefault();
                previewClip();
                break;
            case 'ArrowLeft':
                e.preventDefault();
                if (e.ctrlKey || e.metaKey) {
                    jumpSeconds(-10);
                } else if (e.shiftKey) {
                    jumpSeconds(-1);
                } else {
                    stepFrame(-1);
                }
                break;
            case 'ArrowRight':
                e.preventDefault();
                if (e.ctrlKey || e.metaKey) {
                    jumpSeconds(10);
                } else if (e.shiftKey) {
                    jumpSeconds(1);
                } else {
                    stepFrame(1);
                }
                break;
            case 'Escape':
                e.preventDefault();
                hideKeyboardHelp();
                clearSelection();
                break;
            case '?':
                e.preventDefault();
                showKeyboardHelp();
                break;
        }
    }
    
    // Keyboard help functions
    function showKeyboardHelp() {
        document.getElementById('help-overlay').style.display = 'block';
        document.getElementById('keyboard-help').style.display = 'block';
    }
    
    function hideKeyboardHelp() {
        document.getElementById('help-overlay').style.display = 'none';
        document.getElementById('keyboard-help').style.display = 'none';
    }
    
    function clearSelection() {
        startTime = null;
        endTime = null;
        clipMarker.style.display = 'none';
        startTimeTc.value = '';
        endTimeTc.value = '';
        startTimeInput.value = '';
        endTimeInput.value = '';
        updateTimeDisplays();
    }
    
    // Event listeners for buttons
    document.getElementById('play-pause-btn').addEventListener('click', togglePlayPause);
    document.getElementById('mark-start-btn').addEventListener('click', markIn);
    document.getElementById('mark-end-btn').addEventListener('click', markOut);
    document.getElementById('preview-clip-btn').addEventListener('click', previewClip);
    document.getElementById('keyboard-help-btn').addEventListener('click', showKeyboardHelp);
    
    // Frame control buttons
    document.getElementById('prev-frame-btn').addEventListener('click', () => stepFrame(-1));
    document.getElementById('next-frame-btn').addEventListener('click', () => stepFrame(1));
    document.getElementById('prev-second-btn').addEventListener('click', () => jumpSeconds(-1));
    document.getElementById('next-second-btn').addEventListener('click', () => jumpSeconds(1));
    document.getElementById('prev-10s-btn').addEventListener('click', () => jumpSeconds(-10));
    document.getElementById('next-10s-btn').addEventListener('click', () => jumpSeconds(10));
    
    // Help modal controls
    document.getElementById('close-help-btn').addEventListener('click', hideKeyboardHelp);
    document.getElementById('help-overlay').addEventListener('click', hideKeyboardHelp);
    
    // Timeline click to seek
    timeline.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
        const rect = timeline.getBoundingClientRect();
        const clickPercent = (e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;
        const newTime = clickPercent * videoDuration;
        seekToTime(newTime);
    });
    
    // Timecode input handlers
    startTimeTc.addEventListener('change', function() {
        const newTime = parseTimecode(this.value);
        startTime = newTime;
        startTimeInput.value = Math.floor(newTime);
        updateClipMarker();
        updateTimeDisplays();
    });
    
    endTimeTc.addEventListener('change', function() {
        const newTime = parseTimecode(this.value);
        endTime = newTime;
        endTimeInput.value = Math.floor(newTime);
        updateClipMarker();
        updateTimeDisplays();
    });
    
    // Global keyboard listener
    document.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyboard);
    
    // Form submission
    document.getElementById('clip-form').addEventListener('submit', function(e) {
        e.preventDefault();
        
        // Validate times
        if (startTime === null || endTime === null) {
            alert('Please mark both start and end times.');
            return;
        }
        
        if (startTime >= endTime) {
            alert('End time must be after start time.');
            return;
        }
        
        const formData = new FormData(this);
        
        fetch('{% url "admin:archive_video_create_clip_api" %}', {
            method: 'POST',
            body: formData,
            headers: {
                'X-CSRFToken': document.querySelector('[name=csrfmiddlewaretoken]').value
            }
        })
        .then(response => response.json())
        .then(data => {
            if (data.success) {
                alert(data.message);
                location.reload();
            } else {
                alert('Error: ' + data.error);
            }
        })
        .catch(error => {
            alert('Error creating clip: ' + error);
        });
    });
    
    // Clear form
    document.getElementById('clear-form-btn').addEventListener('click', function() {
        document.getElementById('clip-form').reset();
        clearSelection();
    });
    
    // Simulate time updates (in real implementation, this would come from player events)
    let timeUpdateInterval = setInterval(function() {
        if (isPlaying) {
            currentTime += (1 / videoFPS); // Update by frame
            if (currentTime > videoDuration) {
                currentTime = 0;
                isPlaying = false;
            }
            updateTimeDisplays();
            updateTimelinePosition();
        }
    }, 1000 / videoFPS);
    
    // Initialize
    updateTimeDisplays();
    
    // Show initial help hint
    setTimeout(() => {
        console.log('💡 Press ? for keyboard shortcuts, or use the buttons above the video');
    }, 1000);
});
</script>

## 🎬 Example Frontend Integration

### Upload & Create Clips Workflow
```javascript
// 1. Upload video
const formData = new FormData();
formData.append('file', videoFile);
formData.append('title', 'My Performance');

const uploadResponse = await fetch('/api/videos/upload/', {
  method: 'POST',
  body: formData
});
const video = await uploadResponse.json();

// 2. Wait for processing (poll or webhook)
const refreshResponse = await fetch(`/api/videos/${video.id}/refresh_metadata/`, {
  method: 'POST'
});

// 3. Create clips
const clipResponse = await fetch('/api/clips/', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    video: video.id,
    title: 'Opening Solo',
    start_time_seconds: 120,
    end_time_seconds: 180,
    performers: [1, 2]
  })
});

// 4. Get embed code
const embedResponse = await fetch(`/api/clips/${clip.id}/embed_code/`);
const { embed_code } = await embedResponse.json();
```

### Search & Filter

  performers: '1',
  piece: '2'
});

const clips = await response.json();
```

---

## 🔐 Rate Limits

- **Upload endpoint**: 10 requests/hour per user
- **Other endpoints**: 1000 requests/hour per user
- **Anonymous access**: Not allowed

---

## 📋 Status Codes

- `400` - Bad Request (validation errors)
- `401` - Unauthorized
- `403` - Forbidden
- `404` - Not Found

Performance should have:
- Date

- Company
- Program/repertoire info

Venue should have:

- Location/address
- Capacity

input.content:
# ==============================================================================
# models.py (Enhanced with Performance & Venue)
# ==============================================================================

from django.db import models
from django.core.validators import MinValueValidator
from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError
from datetime import timedelta
import uuid

class Venue(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=255)
    city = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    country = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    venue_type = models.CharField(max_length=50, choices=[
        ('theater', 'Theater'),
        ('opera_house', 'Opera House'),
        ('studio', 'Studio'),
        ('outdoor', 'Outdoor'),
        ('concert_hall', 'Concert Hall'),
        ('other', 'Other'),
    ], default='theater')
    
    capacity = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    address = models.TextField(blank=True)
    founded_year = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    description = models.TextField(blank=True)
    website = models.URLField(blank=True)
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['name']
        unique_together = ['name', 'city', 'country']
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.name}, {self.city}"


class Company(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=255)
    city = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)
    country = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)
    company_type = models.CharField(max_length=50, choices=[
        ('ballet', 'Ballet Company'),
        ('modern', 'Modern Dance Company'),
        ('contemporary', 'Contemporary Dance Company'),
        ('folk', 'Folk Dance Company'),
        ('musical_theater', 'Musical Theater'),
        ('opera', 'Opera Company'),
        ('independent', 'Independent'),
        ('school', 'Dance School'),
        ('other', 'Other'),
    ], default='ballet')
    
    founded_year = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    artistic_director = models.ForeignKey(
        'Person', on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True,
        related_name='directed_companies'
    )
    description = models.TextField(blank=True)
    website = models.URLField(blank=True)
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['name']
        verbose_name_plural = "Companies"
    
    def __str__(self):
        return self.name


class Performance(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=255, help_text="e.g., 'Giselle - Opening Night'")
    date = models.DateField()
    venue = models.ForeignKey(Venue, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='performances')
    company = models.ForeignKey(Company, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='performances')
    
    # Performance details
    season = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True, help_text="e.g., '2023-24 Season'")
    program_notes = models.TextField(blank=True)
    conductor = models.ForeignKey(
        'Person', on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True,
        related_name='conducted_performances'
    )
    stage_director = models.ForeignKey(
        'Person', on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True,
        related_name='directed_performances'
    )
    
    # Performance metadata
    is_premiere = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    is_gala = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    is_matinee = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    performance_time = models.TimeField(null=True, blank=True)
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['-date', 'performance_time']
        unique_together = ['title', 'date', 'venue']
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.title} - {self.venue.name} ({self.date})"


class Person(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=255)
    role = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)  # dancer, choreographer, conductor, etc.
    bio = models.TextField(blank=True)
    birth_year = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    nationality = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)
    
    # Career info
    current_company = models.ForeignKey(
        Company, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True,
        related_name='current_members'
    )
    rank = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True, help_text="e.g., Principal, Soloist, Corps de Ballet")
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['name']
        verbose_name_plural = "People"
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.name} ({self.role})" if self.role else self.name


class Piece(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=255)
    choreographer = models.ForeignKey(Person, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True)
    year_created = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    style = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)  # ballet, modern, jazz, etc.
    description = models.TextField(blank=True)
    
    # Music info
    composer = models.ForeignKey(
        Person, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True,
        related_name='composed_pieces'
    )
    librettist = models.ForeignKey(
        Person, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True,
        related_name='libretto_pieces'
    )
    
    # Structure
    acts = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=1)
    approximate_duration_minutes = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['title']
    
    def __str__(self):
        return self.title


class Video(models.Model):
    id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)
    title = models.CharField(max_length=255)
    description = models.TextField(blank=True)
    
    # Link to performance (replaces old performance fields)
    performance = models.ForeignKey(
        Performance, on_delete=models.CASCADE, 
        related_name='videos',
        null=True, blank=True
    )
    
    # Video type
    video_type = models.CharField(max_length=50, choices=[
        ('full_performance', 'Full Performance'),
        ('dress_rehearsal', 'Dress Rehearsal'),
        ('class', 'Class/Rehearsal'),
        ('behind_scenes', 'Behind the Scenes'),
        ('interview', 'Interview'),
        ('documentary', 'Documentary'),
        ('other', 'Other'),
    ], default='full_performance')
    
    # Cloudflare Stream fields
    cloudflare_stream_id = models.CharField(max_length=255, unique=True)
    cloudflare_playback_url = models.URLField()
    cloudflare_thumbnail_url = models.URLField(blank=True)
    
    # Video metadata
    duration_seconds = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    upload_date = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    file_size_bytes = models.BigIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    
    # Technical details
    camera_angle = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True, help_text="e.g., 'Stage Left', 'Center Orchestra'")
    recording_quality = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=[
        ('4K', '4K'),
        ('HD', 'HD (1080p)'),
        ('SD', 'SD (720p)'),
        ('archival', 'Archival Quality'),
    ], blank=True)
    
    # Status
    STATUS_CHOICES = [
        ('uploading', 'Uploading'),
        ('processing', 'Processing'),
        ('ready', 'Ready'),
        ('error', 'Error'),
    ]
    status = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=STATUS_CHOICES, default='uploading')
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['-created_at']
    
    def __str__(self):
        return self.title
    
    @property
    def duration_formatted(self):
        if not self.duration_seconds:
            return "Unknown"
        return str(timedelta(seconds=self.duration_seconds))
    
    # Legacy properties for backward compatibility
    @property
    def venue(self):
        return self.performance.venue.name if self.performance else ""
    
    @property
    def company(self):
        return self.performance.company.name if self.performance else ""
    
    @property
    def performance_date(self):
        return self.performance.date if self.performance else None


class PerformanceCast(models.Model):
    """Junction table for performance casting"""
    performance = models.ForeignKey(Performance, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='cast')
    person = models.ForeignKey(Person, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='performances')
    piece = models.ForeignKey(Piece, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='cast_entries')
    role = models.CharField(max_length=255, help_text="e.g., 'Giselle', 'Albrecht', 'Queen of the Wilis'")
    is_principal = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    is_understudy = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        unique_together = ['performance', 'person', 'piece', 'role']
        ordering = ['performance', '-is_principal', 'role']
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.person.name} as {self.role} in {self.performance}"


class Clip(models.Model):
    id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)
    video = models.ForeignKey(Video, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='clips')
    
    title = models.CharField(max_length=255)
    description = models.TextField(blank=True)
    
    # Timestamp fields (in seconds)
    start_time_seconds = models.PositiveIntegerField(
        validators=[MinValueValidator(0)]
    )
    end_time_seconds = models.PositiveIntegerField(
        validators=[MinValueValidator(1)]
    )
    
    # Archive-specific metadata
    piece = models.ForeignKey(Piece, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True)
    performers = models.ManyToManyField(Person, blank=True, related_name='clips')
    
    # Clip-specific context (may differ from full performance cast)
    clip_roles = models.JSONField(default=dict, blank=True,

- Video: Just a digitized piece of media
- Clips: The meaningful segments within that video, each potentially from different performances

This is much more realistic for how archives actually work. You find a box of old VHS tapes and digitize them, and each tape might have random stuff recorded on it.

def create_clips_view(self, request, video_id):
        """Custom admin view for creating clips with video player interface"""
        video = get_object_or_404(Video, id=video_id)
        
        if video.status != 'ready':
            messages.error(request, 'Video must be ready before creating clips.')
            return redirect('admin:archive_video_changelist')
        
        clips = video.clips.all().order_by('start_time_seconds')
        people = Person.objects.all()
        pieces = Piece.objects.all()
        performances = Performance.objects.all().order_by('-date')

            'clips': clips,
            'people': people,
            'pieces': pieces,
            'performances': performances,
            'title': f'Archive & Clip - {video.title}',
            'opts': self.model._meta,
            'has_change_permission': True,
            'original': video,
        }
        
        return render(request, 'admin/archive/video_clipping.html', context)

@require_http_methods(["POST"])
    def create_clip_api(self, request):
        """API endpoint for creating clips via AJAX"""
        try:
            video_id = request.POST.get('video_id')
            video = get_object_or_404(Video, id=video_id)
            
            clip = Clip.objects.create(
                video=video,
                title=request.POST.get('title', 'Untitled Clip'),
                description=request.POST.get('description', ''),
                start_time_seconds=int(request.POST.get('start_time')),
                end_time_seconds=int(request.POST.get('end_time')),
                content_type=request.POST.get('content_type', 'unknown'),
                act_scene=request.POST.get('act_scene', ''),
                music_section=request.POST.get('music_section', ''),
                technical_notes=request.POST.get('technical_notes', ''),
                archival_notes=request.POST.get('archival_notes', ''),
                camera_angle=request.POST.get('camera_angle', ''),
                identification_confidence=request.POST.get('identification_confidence', 'uncertain'),
                verified_by=request.user,
                verification_date=timezone.now()
            )
            
            # Add performance if provided
            performance_id = request.POST.get('performance')
            if performance_id:
                clip.performance_id = performance_id
                clip.save()
            
            # Add performers if provided
            performer_ids = request.POST.getlist('performers')
            if performer_ids:
                clip.performers.set(performer_ids)
            
            # Add piece if provided
            piece_id = request.POST.get('piece')
            if piece_id:
                clip.piece_id = piece_id
                clip.save()
            
            # Add movement tags if provided
            movement_tags = request.POST.get('movement_tags', '')
            if movement_tags:
                clip.movement_tags = [tag.strip() for tag in movement_tags.split(',') if tag.strip()]
                clip.save()
            
            return JsonResponse({
                'success': True,
                'clip_id': str(clip.id),
                'message': f'Clip "{clip.title}" created successfully!'
            })
            
        except Exception as e:
            return JsonResponse({
                'success': False,
                'error': str(e)
            }, status=400)

@action(detail=True, methods=['post'])
    def refresh_metadata(self, request, pk=None):
        """Refresh video metadata from Cloudflare Stream"""
        video = self.get_object()
        api = CloudflareStreamAPI()
        
        details = api.get_video_details(video.cloudflare_stream_id)
        if details:
            video.duration_seconds = details.get('duration')
            video.cloudflare_playback_url = details.get('playback', {}).get('hls', '')
            video.cloudflare_thumbnail_url = details.get('thumbnail', '')
            video.status = 'ready' if details.get('status', {}).get('state') == 'ready' else 'processing'
            video.save()
        
        serializer = self.get_serializer(video)
        return Response(serializer.data)

class Clip(models.Model):
    id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)
    video = models.ForeignKey(Video, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='clips')
    
    title = models.CharField(max_length=255)
    description = models.TextField(blank=True)
    
    # Timestamp fields (in seconds)
    start_time_seconds = models.PositiveIntegerField(
        validators=[MinValueValidator(0)]
    )
    end_time_seconds = models.PositiveIntegerField(
        validators=[MinValueValidator(1)]
    )
    
    # THE KEY CHANGE: Performance link is here, not on Video!
    performance = models.ForeignKey(
        Performance, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, 
        null=True, blank=True,
        related_name='clips',
        help_text="Link to specific performance if this clip is from a known performance"
    )
    
    # Content type for this specific clip
    content_type = models.CharField(max_length=50, choices=[
        ('performance', 'Performance'),
        ('rehearsal', 'Rehearsal'),
        ('class', 'Class/Training'),
        ('audition', 'Audition'),
        ('interview', 'Interview'),
        ('behind_scenes', 'Behind the Scenes'),
        ('curtain_call', 'Curtain Call'),
        ('entrance', 'Entrance/Exit'),
        ('unknown', 'Unknown Content'),
    ], default='performance')
    
    # Archive-specific metadata
    piece = models.ForeignKey(Piece, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True)
    performers = models.ManyToManyField(Person, blank=True, related_name='clips')
    
    # Clip-specific context (may differ from full performance cast)
    clip_roles = models.JSONField(default=dict, blank=True, 
                                 help_text="{'person_id': 'role_name'} for this specific clip")
    
    # Archive quality assessment
    archival_quality = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=[
        ('excellent', 'Excellent - High research value'),
        ('good', 'Good - Useful for research'),
        ('fair', 'Fair - Limited research value'),
        ('poor', 'Poor - Documentation only'),
        ('fragment', 'Fragment - Incomplete'),
    ], blank=True)
    
    # Additional metadata
    movement_tags = models.JSONField(default=list, blank=True)  # ["adagio", "arabesque"]
    technical_notes = models.TextField(blank=True)
    costume_notes = models.TextField(blank=True)
    music_notes = models.TextField(blank=True)
    historical_notes = models.TextField(blank=True, help_text="Historical context, significance")
    
    # Research metadata
    transcribed = models.BooleanField(default=False, help_text="Has this been fully documented/transcribed?")
    research_priority = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=[
        ('high', 'High Priority'),
        ('medium', 'Medium Priority'), 
        ('low', 'Low Priority'),
        ('complete', 'Research Complete'),
    ], default='medium')
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['video', 'start_time_seconds']
    
    def clean(self):
        if self.start_time_seconds >= self.end_time_seconds:
            raise ValidationError("End time must be after start time")
        
        if self.video and self.video.duration_seconds:
            if self.end_time_seconds > self.video.duration_seconds:
                raise ValidationError("Clip cannot exceed video duration")
    
    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):
        self.clean()
        super().save(*args, **kwargs)
    
    @property
    def duration_seconds(self):
        return self.end_time_seconds - self.start_time_seconds
    
    @property
    def start_time_formatted(self):
        return str(timedelta(seconds=self.start_time_seconds))
    
    @property
    def end_time_formatted(self):
        return str(timedelta(seconds=self.end_time_seconds))
    
    @property
    def duration_formatted(self):
        return str(timedelta(seconds=self.duration_seconds))
    
    def __str__(self):
        performance_info = f" ({self.performance})" if self.performance else ""
        return f"{self.title}{performance_info} [{self.start_time_formatted}-{self.end_time_formatted}]"

            return None
        
        return upload_info
```

content.text:
**Great question!** This affects how precise your clipping can be.

## **HLS Segmentation Reality:**

HLS streams are chopped into **segments** (usually 6-10 seconds):

```
video.m3u8:
segment-001.ts (0:00 - 0:06)
segment-002.ts (0:06 - 0:12) 
segment-003.ts (0:12 - 0:18)
...
```

## **Seeking Precision:**

### **Traditional HLS:**
You can only seek to **segment boundaries**:
- ❌ Can't seek to exactly 0:07.350
- ✅ Can seek to 0:06.000 or 0:12.000
- **Precision: ±3-5 seconds**

### **Modern Reality (Better):**
**Most browsers** now support **intra-segment seeking**:
- ✅ Browser downloads the segment
- ✅ Seeks within the segment locally
- ✅ Much better precision (frame-level)

## **For Your Archive:**

### **Cloudflare Stream Likely:**
- Uses modern HLS with small segments
- Browser handles intra-segment seeking
- **You'll get frame-level precision for playback**

### **But Timestamp Accuracy:**
```javascript
// This will be precise:
player.currentTime() // 127.433 seconds

// Seeking will work:
player.currentTime(127.433) // Seeks accurately
```

### **Edge Case:**
If you seek to `127.433` but the segment starts at `126.000`, the browser:
1. Downloads segment-021.ts 
2. Seeks to 127.433 within that segment
3. ✅ Works as expected

## **Bottom Line:**
**Modern HLS + Video.js = frame-accurate seeking** for your archival needs. The segment chunking is mostly invisible to your clipping interface.

When you need to study:
- Exact timing of musical beats to movement

- Academic research requiring millisecond accuracy

### **5. Multi-Source Synchronization**
Matching clips to:
- Separate audio recordings
- Multiple camera angles
- Orchestra scores with measure numbers
- **Impact**: Sync drift over time

### **6. Forensic/Legal**
If clips are used for:
- Copyright determinations
- Historical verification 
- Academic citations requiring precise timing
- **Impact**: "Approximately 2:34" isn't good enough

## **🎭 Real Example:**

An archivist wants to clip the exact moment a dancer's foot touches the ground for a technical analysis. HLS might give them the touch ±0.5 seconds, but they need the exact frame.

**For 95% of archival work**: HLS precision is fine.

    function formatTimecode(seconds, includeFrames = true) {
        const totalFrames = Math.floor(seconds * videoFPS);
        const hours = Math.floor(seconds / 3600);
        const minutes = Math.floor((seconds % 3600) / 60);
        const secs = Math.floor(seconds % 60);
        const frames = totalFrames % videoFPS;
        
        if (includeFrames) {
            return `${hours.toString().padStart(2, '0')}:${minutes.toString().padStart(2, '0')}:${secs.toString().padStart(2, '0')}.${frames.toString().padStart(3, '0')}`;
        } else {
            const milliseconds = Math.floor((seconds % 1) * 1000);
            return `${hours.toString().padStart(2, '0')}:${minutes.toString().padStart(2, '0')}:${secs.toString().padStart(2, '0')}.${milliseconds.toString().padStart(3, '0')}`;
        }
    }
    
    function parseTimecode(timecode) {
        // Parse formats: HH:MM:SS.fff, MM:SS.fff, SS.fff, or just seconds
        if (!timecode) return 0;
        
        // If it's just a number, treat as seconds
        if (!isNaN(timecode)) return parseFloat(timecode);
        
        const parts = timecode.split(':');
        let seconds = 0;
        
        if (parts.length === 3) {
            // HH:MM:SS.fff
            seconds = parseInt(parts[0]) * 3600 + parseInt(parts[1]) * 60 + parseFloat(parts[2]);
        } else if (parts.length === 2) {
            // MM:SS.fff
            seconds = parseInt(parts[0]) * 60 + parseFloat(parts[1]);
        } else if (parts.length === 1) {
            // SS.fff
            seconds = parseFloat(parts[0]);
        }
        
        return Math.max(0, Math.min(seconds, videoDuration));
    }
    
    // Update all time displays
    function updateTimeDisplays() {
        const frame = secondsToFrames(currentTime);
        currentTimeDisplay.textContent = formatTimecode(currentTime, false);
        currentFrameDisplay.textContent = frame;
        
        // Update clip duration if both start and end are set
        if (startTime !== null && endTime !== null) {
            const duration = endTime - startTime;
            clipDurationDisplay.textContent = formatTimecode(duration, false);
        } else {
            clipDurationDisplay.textContent = '00:00:00.000';
        }
    }
    
    // Seek to specific time with frame precision
    function seekToTime(newTime) {
        // Round to nearest frame for precision
        const frameTime = framesToSeconds(secondsToFrames(newTime));
        currentTime = Math.max(0, Math.min(frameTime, videoDuration));
        
        // In a real implementation, this would communicate with the Cloudflare player
        // For now, we'll simulate the seeking
        updateTimeDisplays();
        
        // Update timeline position
        updateTimelinePosition();
    }
    
    // Frame-by-frame navigation
    function stepFrame(direction) {
        const currentFrame = secondsToFrames(currentTime);
        const newFrame = Math.max(0, Math.min(currentFrame + direction, secondsToFrames(videoDuration)));
        seekToTime(framesToSeconds(newFrame));
    }
    
    // Jump by seconds
    function jumpSeconds(seconds) {
        seekToTime(currentTime + seconds);
    }
    
    // Update timeline position indicator
    function updateTimelinePosition() {
        if (videoDuration > 0) {
            const percent = (currentTime / videoDuration) * 100;
            // Add a playhead indicator if needed
        }
    }
    
    // Update clip marker on timeline
    function updateClipMarker() {
        if (startTime !== null && endTime !== null && videoDuration > 0) {
            const startPercent = (startTime / videoDuration) * 100;
            const endPercent = (endTime / videoDuration) * 100;
            const width = endPercent - startPercent;
            
            clipMarker.style.left = startPercent + '%';
            clipMarker.style.width = width + '%';
            clipMarker.style.display = 'block';
        }
    }
    
    // Mark in/out points
    function markIn() {
        startTime = currentTime;
        startTimeInput.value = Math.floor(startTime);
        startTimeTc.value = formatTimecode(startTime, false);
        updateClipMarker();
        updateTimeDisplays();
        console.log(`In point marked at ${formatTimecode(startTime)}`);
    }
    
    function markOut() {
        endTime = currentTime;
        endTimeInput.value = Math.floor(endTime);
        endTimeTc.value = formatTimecode(endTime, false);
        updateClipMarker();
        updateTimeDisplays();
        console.log(`Out point marked at ${formatTimecode(endTime)}`);
    }
    
    // Toggle play/pause
    function togglePlayPause() {
        isPlaying = !isPlaying;
        console.log(isPlaying ? 'Playing' : 'Paused');
        // In real implementation, would control the actual video player
    }
    
    // Preview clip
    function previewClip() {
        if (startTime !== null && endTime !== null) {
            const previewUrl = `https://iframe.videodelivery.net/{{ video.cloudflare_stream_id }}?startTime=${Math.floor(startTime)}s`;
            window.open(previewUrl, '_blank');
        } else {
            alert('Please mark both start and end times first.');
        }
    }
    
    // Keyboard event handler
    function handleKeyboard(e) {
        // Don't trigger shortcuts when typing in form fields
        if (e.target.tagName === 'INPUT' || e.target.tagName === 'TEXTAREA' || e.target.tagName === 'SELECT') {
            if (e.key === 'Enter' && e.target.closest('#clip-form')) {
                e.preventDefault();
                document.getElementById('clip-form').dispatchEvent(new Event('submit'));
            }

        switch (e.key) {

            case 'o':

            case 'p':

            case 'ArrowLeft':

                } else {

                }

            case 'ArrowRight':

                } else {

                }

            case 'Escape':
                e.preventDefault();
                hideKeyboardHelp();
                clearSelection();
                break;
            case '?':

                showKeyboardHelp();
                break;
        }
    }
    
    // Keyboard help functions
    function showKeyboardHelp() {

        document.getElementById('keyboard-help').style.display = 'block';
    }
    
    function hideKeyboardHelp() {

        document.getElementById('keyboard-help').style.display = 'none';
    }
    
    function clearSelection() {
        startTime = null;
        endTime = null;
        clipMarker.style.display = 'none';
        startTimeTc.value = '';
        endTimeTc.value = '';
        startTimeInput.value = '';

    // Event listeners for buttons
    document.getElementById('play-pause-btn').addEventListener('click', togglePlayPause);
    document.getElementById('mark-start-btn').addEventListener('click', markIn);
    document.getElementById('mark-end-btn').addEventListener('click', markOut);
    document.getElementById('preview-clip-btn').addEventListener('click', previewClip);
    document.getElementById('keyboard-help-btn').addEventListener('click', showKeyboardHelp);
    
    // Frame control buttons
    document.getElementById('prev-frame-btn').addEventListener('click', () => stepFrame(-1));

    document.getElementById('prev-second-btn').addEventListener('click', () => jumpSeconds(-1));

    document.getElementById('prev-10s-btn').addEventListener('click', () => jumpSeconds(-10));

    // Help modal controls
    document.getElementById('close-help-btn').addEventListener('click', hideKeyboardHelp);
    document.getElementById('help-overlay').addEventListener('click', hideKeyboardHelp);
    
    // Timeline click to seek
    timeline.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
        const rect = timeline.getBoundingClientRect();
        const clickPercent = (e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;
        const newTime = clickPercent * videoDuration;
        seekToTime(newTime);
    });
    
    // Timecode input handlers
    startTimeTc.addEventListener('change', function() {
        const newTime = parseTimecode(this.value);
        startTime = newTime;
        startTimeInput.value = Math.floor(newTime);
        updateClipMarker();
        updateTimeDisplays();
    });
    
    endTimeTc.addEventListener('change', function() {
        const newTime = parseTimecode(this.value);
        endTime = newTime;
        endTimeInput.value = Math.floor(newTime);
        updateClipMarker();
        updateTimeDisplays();
    });
    
    // Global keyboard listener
    document.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyboard);
    
    // Form submission
    document.getElementById('clip-form').addEventListener('submit', function(e) {
        e.preventDefault();
        
        // Validate times
        if (startTime === null || endTime === null) {
            alert('Please mark both start and end times.');
            return;
        }
        
        if (startTime >= endTime) {
            alert('End time must be after start time.');
            return;
        }
        
        const formData = new FormData(this);
        
        fetch('{% url "admin:archive_video_create_clip_api" %}', {
            method: 'POST',
            body: formData,
            headers: {
                'X-CSRFToken': document.querySelector('[name=csrfmiddlewaretoken]').value
            }
        })
        .then(response => response.json())
        .then(data => {
            if (data.success) {
                alert(data.message);
                location.reload();
            } else {
                alert('Error: ' + data.error);
            }
        })
        .catch(error => {
            alert('Error creating clip: ' + error);
        });
    });
    
    // Clear form
    document.getElementById('clear-form-btn').addEventListener('click', function() {
        document.getElementById('clip-form').reset();
        clearSelection();
    });
    
    // Simulate time updates (in real implementation, this would come from player events)
    let timeUpdateInterval = setInterval(function() {
        if (isPlaying) {
            currentTime += (1 / videoFPS); // Update by frame
            if (currentTime > videoDuration) {
                currentTime = 0;
                isPlaying = false;
            }
            updateTimeDisplays();
            updateTimelinePosition();
        }
    }, 1000 / videoFPS);
    
    // Initialize
    updateTimeDisplays();
    
    // Show initial help hint
    setTimeout(() => {
        console.log('💡 Press ? for keyboard shortcuts, or use the buttons above the video');
    }, 1000);
});
</script>

def generate_embed_code(self, video_id: str, start_time: int = None, autoplay: bool = False) -> str:
        """Generate HTML embed code for Cloudflare Stream video"""
        embed_url = f"https://iframe.videodelivery.net/{video_id}"
        
        params = []
        if start_time:
            params.append(f"defaultTextTrack=&poster=&startTime={start_time}s")
        if autoplay:
            params.append("autoplay=true")
        
        if params:
            embed_url += "?" + "&".join(params)
        
        embed_code = f'''
        <iframe
            src="{embed_url}"
            style="border: none; position: absolute; top: 0; height: 100%; width: 100%;"
            allow="accelerometer; gyroscope; autoplay; encrypted-media; picture-in-picture;"
            allowfullscreen="true">
        </iframe>
        '''
        
        return embed_code.strip()

**A few honest reservations:**

## **🧪 Untested Reality:**
We built this assuming Cloudflare Stream HLS URLs work perfectly with Video.js, but **haven't actually tested it**. Could hit:
- CORS blocks on some browsers
- HLS format incompatibilities  
- Cloudflare-specific quirks

## **📱 Mobile/Browser Gaps:**
- **iOS Safari** sometimes has HLS issues with custom players
- **Older browsers** might struggle with Video.js + HLS
- **Mobile touch controls** vs keyboard shortcuts

## **⚡ Performance Trade-offs:**
- **Cloudflare iframe** is probably more optimized (adaptive bitrate, analytics)
- **Video.js** adds ~200KB and complexity
- **Large archive files** (2GB+) might perform worse

## **🎯 Feature Loss:**
We might lose:
- Cloudflare's built-in analytics
- Their optimized mobile experience  
- Automatic quality switching
- Built-in accessibility features

## **🔧 Maintenance Burden:**
- Another dependency to manage
- Video.js version updates
- Browser compatibility testing

## **💡 Mitigation:**
Build **both options** - let archivists choose:
- **Simple iframe** for basic viewing
- **Video.js mode** for precise clipping

**Still think it's worth shipping**, but these are real concerns to monitor in production.

**The core functionality should work great** - just needs real-world validation! 🎬

from django.db import models
from django.core.validators import MinValueValidator
from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError
from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from datetime import timedelta
import uuid

class Venue(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=255)
    city = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    country = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    venue_type = models.CharField(max_length=50, choices=[
        ('theater', 'Theater'),
        ('opera_house', 'Opera House'),
        ('studio', 'Studio'),
        ('outdoor', 'Outdoor'),
        ('concert_hall', 'Concert Hall'),
        ('other', 'Other'),
    ], default='theater')
    
    capacity = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    address = models.TextField(blank=True)
    founded_year = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    description = models.TextField(blank=True)
    website = models.URLField(blank=True)
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['name']
        unique_together = ['name', 'city', 'country']
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.name}, {self.city}"


class Company(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=255)
    city = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)
    country = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)
    company_type = models.CharField(max_length=50, choices=[
        ('ballet', 'Ballet Company'),
        ('modern', 'Modern Dance Company'),
        ('contemporary', 'Contemporary Dance Company'),
        ('folk', 'Folk Dance Company'),
        ('musical_theater', 'Musical Theater'),
        ('opera', 'Opera Company'),
        ('independent', 'Independent'),
        ('school', 'Dance School'),
        ('other', 'Other'),
    ], default='ballet')
    
    founded_year = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    artistic_director = models.ForeignKey(
        'Person', on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True,
        related_name='directed_companies'
    )
    description = models.TextField(blank=True)
    website = models.URLField(blank=True)
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['name']
        verbose_name_plural = "Companies"
    
    def __str__(self):
        return self.name


class Person(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=255)
    role = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)  # dancer, choreographer, conductor, etc.
    bio = models.TextField(blank=True)
    birth_year = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    nationality = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)
    
    # Career info
    current_company = models.ForeignKey(
        Company, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True,
        related_name='current_members'
    )
    rank = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True, help_text="e.g., Principal, Soloist, Corps de Ballet")
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['name']
        verbose_name_plural = "People"
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.name} ({self.role})" if self.role else self.name


class Piece(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=255)

    )

    approximate_duration_minutes = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['title']
    
    def __str__(self):
        return self.title


class Performance(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=255, help_text="e.g., 'Giselle - Opening Night'")
    date = models.DateField()
    venue = models.ForeignKey(Venue, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='performances')
    company = models.ForeignKey(Company, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='performances')
    
    # Performance details
    season = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True, help_text="e.g., '2023-24 Season'")
    program_notes = models.TextField(blank=True)
    conductor = models.ForeignKey(
        Person, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True,
        related_name='conducted_performances'
    )
    stage_director = models.ForeignKey(
        Person, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True,
        related_name='directed_performances'
    )
    
    # Performance metadata
    is_premiere = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    is_gala = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    is_matinee = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    performance_time = models.TimeField(null=True, blank=True)
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['-date', 'performance_time']
        unique_together = ['title', 'date', 'venue']
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.title} - {self.venue.name} ({self.date})"


class PerformanceCast(models.Model):
    """Junction table for performance casting"""
    performance = models.ForeignKey(Performance, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='cast')
    person = models.ForeignKey(Person, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='performances')
    piece = models.ForeignKey(Piece, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='cast_entries')
    role = models.CharField(max_length=255, help_text="e.g., 'Giselle', 'Albrecht', 'Queen of the Wilis'")
    is_principal = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    is_understudy = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    
    class Meta:
        unique_together = ['performance', 'person', 'piece', 'role']
        ordering = ['performance', '-is_principal', 'role']
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.person.name} as {self.role} in {self.performance}"


class Video(models.Model):
    id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)
    title = models.CharField(max_length=255, help_text="e.g., 'VHS Tape #47 - Mixed 1980s Content'")
    description = models.TextField(blank=True, help_text="What we know about this digitized content")
    
    # Archival source info
    source_format = models.CharField(max_length=50, choices=[
        ('vhs', 'VHS'),
        ('betamax', 'Betamax'),
        ('8mm', '8mm Film'),
        ('16mm', '16mm Film'),
        ('digital', 'Digital'),
        ('dvd', 'DVD'),
        ('mini_dv', 'Mini DV'),
        ('hi8', 'Hi8'),
        ('umatic', 'U-matic'),
        ('unknown', 'Unknown'),
    ], default='unknown')
    
    source_condition = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=[
        ('excellent', 'Excellent'),
        ('good', 'Good'),
        ('fair', 'Fair'),
        ('poor', 'Poor'),
        ('degraded', 'Degraded'),
    ], blank=True)
    
    # Provenance
    source_location = models.CharField(max_length=255, blank=True, help_text="Where was this found/acquired?")
    estimated_date_range = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True, help_text="e.g., '1987-1990' or 'Early 1980s'")
    donor_contact = models.CharField(max_length=255, blank=True)
    acquisition_notes = models.TextField(blank=True)
    
    # Cloudflare Stream fields
    cloudflare_stream_id = models.CharField(max_length=255, unique=True)
    cloudflare_playback_url = models.URLField()
    cloudflare_thumbnail_url = models.URLField(blank=True)
    
    # Video metadata
    duration_seconds = models.PositiveIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    upload_date = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    file_size_bytes = models.BigIntegerField(null=True, blank=True)
    
    # Technical details
    recording_quality = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=[
        ('4K', '4K'),
        ('HD', 'HD (1080p)'),
        ('SD', 'SD (720p)'),
        ('archival', 'Archival Quality'),
        ('poor', 'Poor Quality'),
    ], blank=True)
    
    # Processing notes
    digitization_notes = models.TextField(blank=True, help_text="Technical notes about the digitization process")
    needs_review = models.BooleanField(default=True, help_text="Needs archivist review to identify content")
    
    # Status
    STATUS_CHOICES = [
        ('uploading', 'Uploading'),
        ('processing', 'Processing'),
        ('ready', 'Ready'),
        ('error', 'Error'),
    ]
    status = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=STATUS_CHOICES, default='uploading')
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['-created_at']
    
    def __str__(self):
        return self.title
    
    @property
    def duration_formatted(self):
        if not self.duration_seconds:
            return "Unknown"
        return str(timedelta(seconds=self.duration_seconds))


class Clip(models.Model):
    id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False)
    video = models.ForeignKey(Video, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='clips')
    
    title = models.CharField(max_length=255)
    description = models.TextField(blank=True)
    
    # Timestamp fields (in seconds)
    start_time_seconds = models.PositiveIntegerField(
        validators=[MinValueValidator(0)]
    )
    end_time_seconds = models.PositiveIntegerField(
        validators=[MinValueValidator(1)]
    )
    
    # ARCHIVAL INTELLIGENCE - This is where the real metadata lives!
    performance = models.ForeignKey(
        Performance, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True,
        related_name='clips',
        help_text="Which specific performance is this clip from? Leave blank for rehearsals, classes, etc."
    )
    piece = models.ForeignKey(Piece, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True)
    performers = models.ManyToManyField(Person, blank=True, related_name='clips')
    
    # Content type - what kind of clip is this?
    content_type = models.CharField(max_length=50, choices=[
        ('performance', 'Performance'),
        ('rehearsal', 'Rehearsal'),
        ('class', 'Class/Training'),
        ('warmup', 'Warm-up'),
        ('backstage', 'Backstage'),
        ('interview', 'Interview'),
        ('curtain_call', 'Curtain Call'),
        ('intermission', 'Intermission'),
        ('unknown', 'Unknown/Unidentified'),
    ], default='unknown')
    
    # Clip-specific context (may differ from full performance cast)
    clip_roles = models.JSONField(default=dict, blank=True, 
                                 help_text="{'person_id': 'role_name'} for this specific clip")
    
    # Dance-specific metadata
    movement_tags = models.JSONField(default=list, blank=True)  # ["adagio", "arabesque"]
    act_scene = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True, help_text="e.g., 'Act I, Scene 2' or 'Variation III'")
    music_section = models.CharField(max_length=200, blank=True, help_text="e.g., 'Entrance of the Wilis'")
    
    # Technical/archival notes
    technical_notes = models.TextField(blank=True)
    costume_notes = models.TextField(blank=True)
    music_notes = models.TextField(blank=True)
    archival_notes = models.TextField(blank=True, help_text="Archivist notes about identification, context, etc.")
    
    # Quality/condition
    video_quality = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=[
        ('excellent', 'Excellent'),
        ('good', 'Good'),
        ('fair', 'Fair'),
        ('poor', 'Poor'),
        ('very_poor', 'Very Poor'),
    ], blank=True)
    
    audio_quality = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=[
        ('excellent', 'Excellent'),
        ('good', 'Good'),
        ('fair', 'Fair'),
        ('poor', 'Poor'),
        ('very_poor', 'Very Poor'),
        ('no_audio', 'No Audio'),
    ], blank=True)
    
    # Camera/recording details for this specific clip
    camera_angle = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True, help_text="e.g., 'Stage Left', 'Audience POV'")
    
    # Verification status
    identification_confidence = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=[
        ('certain', 'Certain'),
        ('probable', 'Probable'),
        ('possible', 'Possible'),
        ('uncertain', 'Uncertain'),
    ], default='uncertain', help_text="How confident are we in the identification?")
    
    verified_by = models.ForeignKey(
        User, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, blank=True,
        help_text="Which archivist verified this identification?"
    )
    verification_date = models.DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True)
    
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
    
    class Meta:
        ordering = ['video', 'start_time_seconds']
    
    def clean(self):
        if self.start_time_seconds >= self.end_time_seconds:
            raise ValidationError("End time must be after start time")
        
        if self.video and self.video.duration_seconds:
            if self.end_time_seconds > self.video.duration_seconds:
                raise ValidationError("Clip cannot exceed video duration")
    
    def save(self, *args, **kwargs):
        self.clean()
        super().save(*args, **kwargs)
    
    @property
    def duration_seconds(self):
        return self.end_time_seconds - self.start_time_seconds
    
    @property
    def start_time_formatted(self):
        return str(timedelta(seconds=self.start_time_seconds))
    
    @property
    def end_time_formatted(self):
        return str(timedelta(seconds=self.end_time_seconds))
    
    @property
    def duration_formatted(self):
        return str(timedelta(seconds=self.duration_seconds))
    
    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.title} ({self.start_time_formatted}-{self.end_time_formatted})"

import requests
import os
from django.conf import settings
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any

class CloudflareStreamAPI:
    def __init__(self):
        self.account_id = settings.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
        self.api_token = settings.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN
        self.base_url = f"https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/accounts/{self.account_id}/stream"
        self.headers = {
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_token}",
        }
    
    def upload_video(self, file_obj, metadata: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> Optional[Dict]:
        """Upload video file to Cloudflare Stream"""
        url = f"{self.base_url}/direct_upload"
        
        # Create direct upload URL
        upload_data = {
            "maxDurationSeconds": 7200,  # 2 hours max
        }
        if metadata:
            upload_data["meta"] = metadata
        
        response = requests.post(
            url,
            headers=self.headers,
            json=upload_data
        )
        
        if response.status_code != 200:
            return None
        
        upload_info = response.json()["result"]
        upload_url = upload_info["uploadURL"]
        
        # Upload the actual file
        files = {"file": file_obj}
        upload_response = requests.post(upload_url, files=files)
        
        if upload_response.status_code != 200:
            return None
        
        return upload_info
    
    def get_video_details(self, video_id: str) -> Optional[Dict]:
        """Get video details from Cloudflare Stream"""
        url = f"{self.base_url}/{video_id}"
        response = requests.get(url, headers=self.headers)
        
        if response.status_code == 200:
            return response.json()["result"]
        return None
    
    def get_hls_url(self, video_id: str) -> str:
        """Get HLS manifest URL for Video.js"""
        # Extract customer code from account ID or use a default pattern
        customer_code = self.account_id[:8] if len(self.account_id) >= 8 else "m033z5x00ks6nunl"
        return f"https://customer-{customer_code}.cloudflarestream.com/{video_id}/manifest/video.m3u8"
    
    def generate_embed_code(self, video_id: str, start_time: int = None, use_iframe: bool = True) -> str:
        """Generate HTML embed code for Cloudflare Stream video"""
        
        if use_iframe:
            # Cloudflare iframe embed (traditional)
            embed_url = f"https://iframe.videodelivery.net/{video_id}"
            
            params = []
            if start_time:
                params.append(f"defaultTextTrack=&poster=&startTime={start_time}s")
            
            if params:
                embed_url += "?" + "&".join(params)
            
            embed_code = f'''
            <iframe
                src="{embed_url}"
                style="border: none; position: absolute; top: 0; height: 100%; width: 100%;"
                allow="accelerometer; gyroscope; autoplay; encrypted-media; picture-in-picture;"
                allowfullscreen="true">
            </iframe>
            '''
        else:
            # Video.js embed with HLS (better control)
            hls_url = self.get_hls_url(video_id)
            
            start_param = f"#t={start_time}" if start_time else ""
            
            embed_code = f'''
            <link href="https://vjs.zencdn.net/8.6.1/video-js.css" rel="stylesheet">
            <video
                id="archive-video-{video_id}"
                class="video-js vjs-default-skin"
                controls
                preload="auto"
                width="800"
                height="450"
                data-setup='{{}}'>
                <source src="{hls_url}{start_param}" type="application/x-mpegURL">
                <p class="vjs-no-js">To view this video please enable JavaScript.</p>
            </video>
            <script src="https://vjs.zencdn.net/8.6.1/video.min.js"></script>
            <script>
                videojs('archive-video-{video_id}', {{
                    responsive: true,
                    fluid: true,
                    playbackRates: [0.5, 1, 1.5, 2]
                }});
            </script>
            '''
        
        return embed_code.strip()
